Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

content.text: I've gathered the relevant project status, testing, risk, and planning documents. Writing the readiness assessment now.

input.content:
Demo Readiness Assessment — Helios for Meridian Group
Demo date: 2026-07-25 (confirmed) | Assessment based on current sandbox materials

We are not fully ready to demo Helios to Meridian Group in two weeks. The project is broadly on track and several core pieces are in good shape—the API gateway is complete, authentication flows pass testing, and basic CSV/JSON/XML ingestion and data-table rendering work. However, the demo is expected to show live data ingestion with Meridian’s data and real dashboard visualization (per the Q3 roadmap and 2026-07-08 sync notes), and critical path items remain open that put that requirement at risk.

The highest-priority issues to resolve first are: (1) date format normalization for MM/DD/YYYY Meridian source data (HEL-D-014 / Blocker #1 / HEL-243)—currently failing and blocked pending Meridian’s clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15; this affects roughly 40% of their data and is severity HIGH; (2) GeoSync v2.1 license renewal (R-02 / HEL-248), still in legal review with a target of 2026-07-14—without it, mapping-related demo paths may be unavailable; and (3) large-file ingestion memory overflow (HEL-D-015), which crashes the pipeline around 210k records and undermines a credible live-ingestion demo. Dashboard work is only about 60% complete: chart rendering is still in progress or not run, and the performance benchmark for large datasets (HEL-247) has not started, despite explicit Meridian concern about load times.

Secondary gaps that should be closed or clearly scoped out of the demo: incomplete integration testing (overall ~40%; several dashboard and load tests not run), overdue integration test suite setup and performance benchmarking report, and UAT environment provisioning still at risk and blocked on Meridian IT access. Sprint 14 goals explicitly include completing dashboard v1 and resolving the data-format blocker before UAT prep—both remain unfinished. If the sample data arrives on time (7/15), the date fix and license renew by mid-next week, and we either fix or carefully limit large-file and chart scope, a focused live demo on 7/25 is still achievable. Without those resolutions, we should not commit to a full live-ingestion and full-dashboard demo.

content.text: Saved the assessment as `demo_readiness_assessment.txt`. It concludes we are not fully ready: live Meridian data ingestion and dashboard visualization are required for the 2026-07-25 demo, but date-format handling (blocked on Meridian sample data), GeoSync license renewal, large-file ingestion crashes, and incomplete dashboard/performance work still need to be resolved first.
